title: JavaScript Runtime, Tooling, and Evidence · Async Failure Triage
summary: Trace a small service-status script from source through parsing, execution, console evidence, and a user-visible result. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a reproducible service-status runtime notebook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Distinguish ECMAScript language behavior from browser, worker, and server host APIs.
Trace source parsing, module instantiation, execution contexts, jobs, and observable output.
Use console evidence, breakpoints, scope inspection, watch expressions, and stack traces without relying on logging alone.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Reproducible Environment evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Reproducible Environment assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Normalize service inputs [" 6 ", 6, null, {value:6}] without implicit-coercion surprises. Record typeof, equality, parsed value, and the exact branch that rejects the object case.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Reproducible Environment has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Reproducible Environment precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Normalize service inputs [" 6 ", 6, null, {value:6}] without implicit-coercion surprises. Record typeof, equality, parsed value, and the exact branch that rejects the object case.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: The same script behaves identically in every JavaScript host.

options.text: Declare inputs, observable output, ownership, and the runtime behavior being tested
Trace values, bindings, receivers, mutations, and queued work before editing code
Implement the smallest behavior and retain an assertion over the actual result
Run boundary and changed-event cases, inspect failures, and remove accidental coupling
